title: malus caspiriensis
intro:
Malus caspica is actually the botanical name of a shrub species that is native to parts of Europe and Asia. However, I couldn't find information on a plant called Malus caspiriensis.

However, I can tell you about Malus caspica. Malus caspica, also known as the Caspian crabapple, is a type of deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Malus caspica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. Malus caspica can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion for its medicinal benefits.
